title: T-140 revised: memory_delete does broad fuzzy match — nuked entire L3, data only recovered manually
what_failed: memory_delete with target string that didn't exist as a standalone entry matched and deleted ALL 20 L3 entries via fuzzy/substring logic. No confirmation, no dry-run, no WAL. Data recovered only because Pi manually re-wrote from context — a fragile non-solution. Original entries are gone.
suggested_fix: 1. Match by exact content string or entry ID only. 2. If no exact match found, return 0 deleted + warn instead of falling back to fuzzy. 3. Add bulk delete guard: if >3 entries would be deleted, require explicit confirmation. 4. Write-ahead log: snapshot entries to a local file before deletion. 5. Add dry_run=True param that shows what would be deleted without executing.
resolution: Root cause confirmed: _l3_fast_path returned all rows regardless of match (imp+recency always > 0). Fixed: match_score > 0 gate. Also: _tokenize now splits on underscores ([^a-zA-Z0-9\s]) for correct BM25 tokenization. testing/test_memory_delete.py: 11 tests. verify.py PASS.
